description: You are a build-and-release engineer working inside a project workspace located at /home/user/build. What already exists 1. /home/user/build/artifacts.json • JSON array of three artifact objects (see structure details below). 2. /home/user/build/artifact_schema.json • A JSON-Schema draft-07 document that describes the required shape of every artifact object (name, version, sha256). Goal Create two new files derived from artifacts.json by issuing a single, well-crafted terminal command that pipes artifacts.json through jq. A. /home/user/build/filtered_artifacts.json • MUST contain only the artifacts whose “version” field starts with the string “1.2.”. • The file must be valid JSON, pretty-printed with an indentation of exactly two spaces. • The order of the objects must be the same as in the original artifacts.json. B. /home/user/build/filter.log • A plain-text file containing one and only one line, literally: Filtered 2 artifacts Constraints & verification rules (follow precisely; automated grading is strict) 1. Use jq’s regex filtering capability (do NOT hand-edit the JSON). 2. filtered_artifacts.json must be byte-for-byte identical to the reference answer the grader expects (pretty-printing, spacing, newlines, and field order matter). 3. filter.log must consist of the single line shown above, terminated by a single trailing newline. Hints • The jq expression will look roughly like: map(select(.version | test("^1\\.2\\."))) but write the full command yourself. • You can create both files in one compound command by exploiting shell redirection techniques. • The schema file is present only for context; you do not need to touch it. Outcome When you are done: • /home/user/build/filtered_artifacts.json and /home/user/build/filter.log exist with the exact contents described. The grader will read these two files to decide success.
